concerns.text: FR-005 requires integration of explicit solvent models (QM/MM or cluster-continuum) for ≥20% of conditions. Tasks T029-T035 only cover implicit models (SMD/PCM) parsing and correlation. No task exists to generate, parse, or integrate explicit solvent model data, leaving a mandatory functional requirement unimplemented.

FR-002 and US-1 require capturing transient-absorption data. T015 generates synthetic mock data. While the plan mentions synthetic data for validation, there is no task to implement the interface or logic for ingesting/processing REAL instrument traces as required by FR-002 ('System MUST capture...'). The implementation relies entirely on mocks, failing the spec requirement.

FR-006 requires statistical significance testing (ANOVA) across solvent conditions. T030 performs linear regression and bootstrapped CIs, and T032 applies multiple-comparison correction, but no task explicitly implements ANOVA or the specific significance testing framework mandated by FR-006.

SC-003 requires performing significance testing and reporting exact p-values. T030 and T034 mention p-values, but the Plan (Phase 3) explicitly states 'Avoid p-value significance testing due to low N'. There is no task that resolves this conflict by implementing the required ANOVA/p-value reporting while adhering to the plan's constraints, leaving the success criterion unaddressed.

SC-004 requires environmental parameters logged within tolerance for ≥95% of runs. T014 logs parameters and T018 flags outliers, but no task explicitly calculates the compliance percentage or implements the logic to verify the ≥95% threshold against the logged data as required by the success criterion.

T015 description states 'generating synthetic transient-absorption data... ensuring no inherent correlation'. This phrasing is ambiguous regarding the spec's requirement for real data capture. While likely intended for the 'null hypothesis' test, the task description should explicitly clarify that this is for the 'mock' scenario only, distinct from the FR-002 real-data requirement, to avoid confusion.
